description: Apollo 17 Flight Journal - Day 6, part 2: Preparations for landing

tokens: 110:39:18 - This is Apollo Control; 110 hours, 39 minutes Ground Elapsed Time in the mission of Apollo 17.
Less than 2 minutes remaining now until America and Challenger, separated and flying a few feet apart at this time, will come around the front side of the Moon on the twelfth lunar orbit.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Slightly over a minute now until we should start attempting to lock up on the downlink signal from America and Challenger.
The two-way communications from spacecraft communicator for the Command Service Module, Ken Mattingly, and Ron Evans aboard America will be on a separate loop as it was the last revolution.
These conversations can be heard in room 161 in the Houston News Center.
Early in this revolution we'll have the pressurization of the Descent Propulsion System and checkout of that system.
We'll stand by until Network advises the Flight Director that they do have firm lock-on.
We have AOS in Lunar Module, let's see what we hear.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Challenger [garble] checkout is complete [garble] and we're looking at America, the beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Geno, I understand you're undocked.
But we're not reading, but about 10 percent of what you're saying.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: The residuals on the P41 were plus 1.9, minus 0.2, and 0.
Undock and sep was on time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Won't hold on the steerable antenna yet.
It looks like I'm getting oscillations in my uplink signal strength, and then it gradually drops off to zero.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you're reading, you got the words.
Landing radar self test was Go.
We're ready to press on to the DPS throttle check, and we've been looking at America, the beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and the residuals on P47 at undocking were 0, minus 0.1, and 0.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like you to try the steerable again.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You sound real good; loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, let me give you some Noun 20 angles, if you want them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Noun 20 from the computer yields the gimbal angles from the IMU.
They therefore represent the spacecraft's attitude with respect to whatever REFSMMAT the IMU platform is aligned to.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The LM: plus 301.09, plus 284.53, plus 359.48; the CSM: plus three zeros .35, plus 104.67, plus 000.52; the time: 110:24:00.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm ready to hit Engine Stop.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ]We're showing the Engine Arm circuit breaker may be out.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: A little - We missed that here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you have an opportunity, how about cycling the High Gain to Wide and back to Reacq?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: How about going back to Narrow on the antenna.
And could you verify what you loaded on Noun 34?
It went by so fast on the downlink, we didn't get it a chance to look at it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Gordy, if the throttle test looked okay, I'll go Engine Arm Off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ALSJ -Cernan and Schmitt pressurized the propellant tanks and, a few minutes later, made their last pass over Taurus-Littrow prior to the descent.
Evans had not yet made his circularization burn and was ahead and slightly below them.
It was a spectacular, oblique view of the valley.
They were less than fifteen miles away, and the LM crew took a couple of color pictures.
Cernan was particularly interested in the small group of craters that surrounded his target point, and he called out the names as he found them.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, Gordy, the Master Arm is coming on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gordy, there was a slight upward - Gordy, there was an upward fluctuation in pressure in the manifold when we fired that.
It's back to where it was, prefiring.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's what it should have done, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay; looks good on the ground.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Say, Gordy, this thing sounds a little bit like my stomach sounded a couple of days ago.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, 10 [garble] 110:52:00 for the time on the AGS.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Geno, we show Jack's Suit Isol valve in Suit Disconnect.
Would you check that for - for us, please?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'll tell you - I'll tell you, this LCG sure makes a world of difference up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This ridge you're coming on over - just stick your hand out the hatch and grab a rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like it's pretty low down there.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Well, when you're up here looking at where you are, it even looks lower.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I have a new AGS K factor for you.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: It is absolutely spectacular looking at that Command Module America down there coming across the surface.
We're just tracking him at about a 30-degree dive angle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, babe; have a good time, and go get that landmark.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: TEI or Trans-Earth Injection would mean that Ron is heading for home.
Gene and Jack would rather he didn't do so until they return from the surface in three days.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Geno, Houston, with a couple of items.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Hey, we got the landing site, Gordo.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gordo, we got the landing site.
We're coming right over the front of it.
I think you can see the Great Cross.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll get a picture of America coming right across it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gosh, we've got Family Mountain; we've got - of course - the Massif; we can see the Scarp; we can see the light mantle; I've got the Great Cross, Camelot, Sherlock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Believe it or not, Houston, they're all there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I see possible structure - possible structure in the upper part of the South Massif, little bit east of Station 2.
It's subhorizontal, dipping to the southeast.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Houston, I can even see Poppy, right where we're going to set this baby down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As a matter of fact, I can see Rudolph.
I can even see the triangle: Rudolph, Frosty, and Punk.
[Pause.] Man, Gordo, this is absolutely spectacular.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We can watch Ron track - we can watch Ron track right on through the landmarks.
I don't know what kind of results he got, but he sure had a nice smooth track from here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gordo, you can go ahead and update us with those words.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: PDI will be a little higher than nominal: 10.7 miles or 65,000 feet [19.8 km]; should be no problem.
And, from the time you first came around until we had a solid lockup on the steerable on this acquisition was about 3 minutes.
We're going to try to speed that up some on the next time around.
We'd like you to just keep trying the steerable until we come to you - and say, "stop trying." Over.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordy, understand that; and apparently this time, had I let it - had I waited a little longer, it would have dropped to zero and then come up, because that's what happened when I finally got you.
I'll give it more time next time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, I've got lots of PADs for you whenever you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordy; go with the PADs.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The first one is a P76 with a CSM circ.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Noun 33 is 111:57:30.09; Noun 84: plus 0070.5, plus five zeros, and minus 0000.5.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: 111:57:30.09; plus 0070.5, plus all zeros, minus four zeros 5.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Next one I have is the No-PDI plus 12 abort, item Echo.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Echo is 113:02:00.00; Foxtrot, plus 0103.4, plus five zeros, minus 0050.0; Noun 42: 0142.0, plus 0005.4, 0114.9; burn time is 0:48; 000, 272; 373...
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Give me a transponder, and we'll start with the radar.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 373 is 0182.0; AGS Delta-Vs: plus 0103.7, plus five zeros, minus 0049.3; Golf, 113:57:00.00; Hotel, 11 - I'll start over - 115:36:45.00; and the No-DOI-2 Delta-VX, 0096.6.
Two remarks: throttle profile is 10 percent for 26 seconds, 40 percent for the rest of the burn.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: 11 - No-PDI plus 12 - 113:02 all zeros; plus 0103.4, plus all zeros, minus 0050.0; 0142.0, plus three zeros 5.4, 0114.9; 0:48; all zeros, 272; 0182.0; plus 0103.7, plus all zeros, minus 0049.3; 113:57 all zeros; 115:36:45.00; 0096.6; remarks: throttle profile 10 percent for 26 seconds; 40 percent for the remainder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We want to finish the radar VHF test; and when I go to P52, you can finish the PADs.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're in VHF Ranging, and I've got you on radar, Ron.
We'll be quiet for a second and see if you can get a lockon.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The VHF ranging and radar checks out very well.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Tell me when you're ready for item India again.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, I'm going to turn the Ranging, Off, then.
The comm's a little better that way.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Lima is 113:14:24.91; Maytro, 119:34:30.00; and November is 114:57:19.09; And your T-2 at PDI - T-2 will be at PDI plus 24:33.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Linda is 113:14:24.91; Mary is 119:34:30.00, and Nancy is 114:57:19.09.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, one thing left, Jack, is the SHe pressures on the PDI page.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay; at TIG, the pressure will be 1310; plus 1 minute, 1410; 2 minutes, 1400; 3 minutes, 1310; and 4 minutes, 1230.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: [Pause.] You look just as pretty in earthlight as you do in sunlight.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but it's not an easy one, Gordo.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Jack, can you go to Receive only on your VHF there?
I've got all these PADs to pick up now.
I'll call you when - when I'm all through.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Challenger, we need Aft Omni and select the steerable to Slew.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordo, the COAS alignment's good and the DAP is reset.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and like the rest of the spacecraft, the platform's beautiful, there's no drift compensation or PIPA bias update.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Like to give you one update, you can write it in the Timeline, page 8; the T-1 time is PDI plus 17:00.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay; T-1 is PDI plus 17:00, we got it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Houston, you got P00 and Data.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gordo, can I start maneuvering to the AGS cal while you're getting those updates - uplinks ready?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we'd like the steerable back again.
Try Pitch of minus 25, and Yaw of minus 72.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, we need High bit rate.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We'll get that in before we go to the AGS cal attitude.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordo, I've got - I've got the Earth and the direction I have to maneuver is nothing but good for the high gain, so I'll start over slowly.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I just wanted to make sure - voice check if we still have the VHF comm.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, I'll have to give you another one here in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, America, we show you inside of 20 minutes, saying you have a Go for circ.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Thank you, Gordo, We're Go here for DOI-2.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Houston, did you get the AGS cal numbers?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, it all looks pretty good to me.
546 may have been a little more than specs, but it looks pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, have you got to an Omni?
If you have, go Low Bit Rate.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, We've got you on an Omni and Low Bit...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've had Loss of Signal as America and Challenger, coasting along nearby each other, passed behind the Moon on revolution number 12.
Some 47 minutes until they come back around again on the 13th lunar revolution.

Behind the Moon this time, the Command Service Module, piloted by Ron Evans, will do the CSM circularization maneuver which will place the spacecraft back up into an almost circular lunar orbit, measuring 69.7 by 54.5 [nautical miles, 129.1 by 100.1 km], those are the targeted measurements of that orbit.
Ignition time, again which will be behind the Moon and out of contact, is Ground Elapsed Time of 111:57:28.
The Lunar Module DOI, or Descent Orbit Insertion burn number 2 also will be behind the Moon before we reacquire the spacecraft.
That is scheduled for 112 hours, 2 minutes, 40 seconds.

It's a fairly small burn of 7.6 feet per second, which will lower the pericynthion of Challenger down to 6½ miles - nautical miles [12.0 km] with apocynthion staying at 61.5 nautical miles [113.9 km].

During the next front side pass, comes the moment of truth with powered descent, and touchdown at Taurus-Littrow landing site as Challenger lights off the big descent engine at 112 hours, 49 minutes, 52 seconds Ground Elapsed Time.

The ignition countdown clock in the control center is counting down to PDI, some 59 minutes, 17 - 16 seconds until ignition with landing shortly under 12 minutes later, depending on the amount of hover time available.
The broadcast line will be brought up again just prior to Acquisition of Signal as America and Challenger come around on the front side again on revolution 13.
And at 111 hours, 51 minutes; this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: ALSJ -Evans did his circularization burn and, five minutes later, the LM crew did a more modest burn to move the low point of their orbit down to 10½ miles over a point east of the landing site.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: 112:33:34 - This is Apollo Control at 112 hours, 33 minutes Ground Elapsed Time in the mission of Apollo 17.
Coming up on Acquisition of Signal of both spacecraft, America and Challenger, coming from behind the Moon on the 13th lunar orbit.
Some 1 minute, 32 seconds away from acquisition of the Command Module and slightly under three minutes on the Lunar Module, Challenger.
Challenger, during this revolution will descend to the surface of the Moon and land at the Taurus-Littrow landing site.
Ignition time currently is Ground Elapsed Time of 112 hours, 49 minutes, 52 seconds.

During the descent to the surface the spacecraft onboard computer will be generating numbers on the display which the Lunar Module Pilot will call out to the commander and which - angles at which the commander should be able to see the landing site through a grid etched on the left hand window of the Lunar Module.
AOS of the Command Service Module and the circulars - the circularization burn was good.
This is Apollo Control on the air-ground 2 circuit.
Command Module Pilot Ron Evans is giving the CapCom all of the residuals.
Back to the lunar landing sequence as the Lunar Module Pilot reads out the angles that the Commander ought to be able to see the landing site.
The Commander is able to run through an operation called redesignation by using a pitch hand controller to rotate the spacecraft.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: ALSJ -At AOS on the 13th orbit, Evans reappeared first and confirmed that the LM burn had gone well.
And then, once Houston had a bit more tracking data with which to update the LM computer, everything was ready for the landing.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, Challenger, you're loud and clear on the Omni.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We're in a 7-mile perigee on the PGNS, and we had 0, plus 0.1 and plus 0.1 residuals.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Ok - Okay, Gordy, I'm going to try the high gain I had you locked up once, and then I lost you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I didn't know you were uplinking.
You've got the Omni, and I'll leave it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we'll stay on the Omni for the uplink.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordy, ED Bats are 37.2, both batteries.
The ascent battery On time was 112:19:00, about 4 minutes late.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, we want Battery 3, Off for preconditioning.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Challenger, we'd like you to verify that the...
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And do you have a 231 update?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Gordo, how do you read CDR on Vox?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: CDR, you're loud and clear on Vox.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: How do you read the LMP on Vox?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gordo, up until this time, the bird has looked beautiful - perfectly plain.
All the check's have come out just as advertised.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we're looking at 9 minutes and 5 seconds from PDI.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Challenger, do you see a Verb 33 out of DSKY?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, it's there, and I will Enter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, it took; and I'm showing P00.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we've got a tone on the upvoice backup.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Verb 47 coming in at you, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, 400 plus 3, and I'll watch it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's - it's had that all the way along - a little bit of roll bias.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and we do have your needle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and there's Verb 83 looking at you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My Crosspointers are Low Mult for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like you to try the high gain once more.
Pitch is minus - Pitch is zero and Yaw plus 59.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Are you happy with this, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gordy, understand no Noun 68 prior to P63 or Noun 69, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: [Garble] you need to ask him anything, I'll try the high gain.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, it's locked up in Auto.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Gordy, be advised that you're clipping on your first word.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like you to set 410 in the AGS to all balls.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You better go back and check 400 now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, man, are we down among them, babe!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Noun 61 cross range should be a plus 2.8.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Say the seconds again on the PDI.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Cross range is - is a plus 2.8.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: That clock checks with - with our time out of P63.
The Landing Radar breaker's In, I've got altitude, velocity, power.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll give you the final trim at 4.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We are Go up here for PDI; doing the final trim at 4.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yes, that's a good point, I'll go ahead and get it.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Hey, Jack, you can check your watch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: At 2 minutes, I'll get the Master Arm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 30 seconds, I'll get the Engine Arm; and we'll watch the PGNS tapemeter pick up average G.
If you give me a Go on the ullage, I'll back up the ullage.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll back up the Start.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Challenger, should we lose the steerable,...
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, we picked it all up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Coming up on 2 minutes [to Powered Descent Initiation, PDI]; I'm changing over here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Once again, in average G, I'll get the Engine Arm (switch).
You confirm the ullage, I'll get the Pro.
I'll back up the ullage and get the Start.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Challenger, we're going to leave Batt(ery) 3, Off...
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Yeah, I should have put that on like we talked about.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: In just under two minutes, Gene and Jack will begin their descent to the surface when the LM's descent engine ignites for its long burn.
The progress of the surface mission can be followed in the Apollo 17 Lunar Surface Journal (link below).
This flight journal continues with the solo mission of Ron Evans in orbit around the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Commentary
